name: USGS Spectral Library v7
spectral_type: Broad reference library: minerals, soils, vegetation, man-made materials, liquids/water, frozen volatiles
coverage: 0.2-200 um overall; USGS also distributes an ASD-convolved 350-2500 nm product
notes: Preferred ingest target is ASCIIdata_splib07b_cvASD.zip (spectra convolved to ASD standard resolution); acquire a fresh signed ScienceBase download URL at build time.

name: ECOSTRESS Spectral Library v1.0
spectral_type: Broad reference library: vegetation, NPV, rocks, soils, snow/ice, man-made materials
notes: Direct bundle checkout is cart-based; fetch raw ASCII spectra from ecospeclibdata by enumerating filenames through the library search endpoint.

name: ASTER Spectral Library v2.0
spectral_type: Broad legacy reference library: minerals, rocks, soils, vegetation, snow/ice, man-made materials
notes: Catalog link points to documentation PDF; resolve actual downloadable assets before fetch.

name: Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL)
coverage: Vis-NIR typically 350-2500 nm plus MIR holdings
notes: Includes MIR holdings; use only reflectance-compatible Vis-NIR subset in core product.

name: ICRAF Spectral Library
spectral_type: Soil-dominant library with additional sediments, plants, some man-made materials and rocks
notes: Website landing page likely requires a source-specific downloader or manual asset resolution.

name: Brazilian Soil Spectral Library (BSSL)
coverage: Vis-NIR-SWIR: 2151 bands / 16,084 rows; MIR subset also included
notes: Use Vis-NIR-SWIR reflectance records first; keep MIR as raw-only until separate handling exists.

name: LUCAS Soil spectral data
coverage: Vis-NIR 400-2500 nm
notes: Catalog link is a contextual ESDAC page; confirm the exact downloadable dataset and terms before fetch.

name: Vis-NIR Soil Spectral Library of the Hungarian Soil Degradation Observation System (HSDOS)
ProbeField pre-processed spectra
notes: Excluded from curated merged database by source curation rule.

name: ProbeField aligned spectra
coverage: Soil laboratory spectra; aligned using internal soil standard
notes: Already aligned across instruments; treat as derived support data, not primary raw spectra. Excluded from curated merged database by source curation rule.

name: Near-infrared soil spectral library using the NeoSpectra scanner
coverage: Narrower NIR range than classic ASD-style VSWIR
notes: Range is narrower than the 400-2500 nm core grid; expect sparse coverage mask.

name: Spectral library of vegetation from Mediterranean woodlands
coverage: Vegetation reflectance library from Mediterranean oak woodland

name: Spectral library of plant species from Montesinho Natural Park
coverage: Species-level reflectance database

name: Drylands spectral libraries in support of EMIT

name: NGEE Arctic 2018 Vegetation Endmember Spectral Reflectance

name: Global Hyperspectral Imaging Spectral-library of Agricultural crops for Conterminous United States V001 (GHISACONUS)
coverage: Hyperion-based crop library for major CONUS crops and growth stages

name: Global Hyperspectral Imaging Spectral-library of Agricultural crops for Central Asia V001 (GHISACASIA)
coverage: Hyperion + field ASD crop library for Central Asia irrigated agriculture

name: Spectral Library of Impervious Urban Materials (SLUM)

coverage: Adjusted from USGS v7 and related source spectra

name: EMIT manually adjusted surface reflectance spectra
coverage: Heavily altered from original measurements for atmospheric-correction statistics

name: earthlib
coverage: 400-2450 nm at 10 nm band widths; vegetation, soil, NPV, urban, burned materials
notes: Already harmonized to 10 nm; useful for validation but not as a primary raw ingest source.
